Other notable characters are Aigis, sorel, E1 horla, and murphy. I don't have many non limiteds but I can get two of those selectors.

will rlly appreciate advice!

thread_title: Help on the Soul Rewind core? New player

content:
Save the core until you need it for sure. Your team should work just fine and cores are very valuable.

Make sure to grab Saya's wheel if you haven't done so already. It is really strong. I would also grab Clementine and Hameln's wheel, even if you leave them at just e0. If, after that, you are itching to pull for more, e0 hameln or e2 saya is not a bad idea imo. E0 Hameln is a pretty decent support that works fine without dupes, and e2 Saya helps a lot with embryo gen. 

This is a (fairly safe) prediction, but biav will probably rerun all characters again for the 3rd anniversary. If you like the castor+pollux team, there's no reason not to save pulls and get them then. Wanting arc 2 limiteds means waiting for their banner to rerun or for anni, at the end of the day.
thread_title: Help on the Soul Rewind core? New player

content:
Sounds good, thanks!

also, when you say save the core until I absolutely need it, would that be for example when I'm one unit/copy away from making a good team?
thread_title: Help on the Soul Rewind core? New player

content: Not even that, I would argue to only use it after trying the team while missing said component, and being sure you need the dupe or unit

thread_title: New player looking for advice

content:
If you find it boring stop playing it altogether, there are plenty of teams that work perfectly fine and things will only get more mind numbing as mechanics become more complex and bosses start hitting harder and harder. Check Cheri's guide and her sections on team building if you are curious about how teambuilding works.

You also don't have to pull on the blue banner by any means. All realms work really well and the majority of standard units have a place where they shine. My recommendation would be to check which standards you like the most, and check teammate recommendations in Cheri's guide.

Saya does a lot of things, but the main reason she's busted is because of her 0 cost card. Discarding and redrawing your hand for free is great, but the main selling point is the corrosion. Corrosion is a stackable debuff that, when the enemy receives damage, gets reduced by the dealt amount and then deals 3x the damage. Think of it as a 4x damage multiplier. She also brings propagation caro, which essentially means her embryos, besides giving aliemus, give a one time per turn buff to the unit's exalt. From my experience, the amount tends to be around a +40% value increase, which is great for awakeners whose main function is to exalt as often as possible. She also creates copies of another unit's cards and cheat their cost through her exalt (duped cards stay in the deck between fights until used), and her rouse is a pretty strong aliemus battery with an arith cheating mechanic, albeit with a couple caviats.

Overall, Saya is a busted support and you should use her unless you dislike her gameplay. She also pairs well with Thais. You're out of luck with Arachne though, she is an arc2 limited and her banner was pretty recently, so you'll most likely have to wait until 3rd anni for her rerun.
thread_title: New player looking for advice

content:
Horla is a really strong support. While it is a shame you got rng'd, you should consider putting her in your team in that case. I would suggest you get Clementine, who's currently on rotation. Not only is she a really strong support, but she can even perform as a dps at e2-e3, even more so if paired with Saya. I've played Saya e1/Clem e3/Horla e1 on some of the hardest stages of arc1 and I can attest it goes hard.

Be cautious about youtube guides, a particular big morimens youtuber is well known in this community for his biased, old and meme-y takes, besides his pretty damning attitude.

I'll link Cheri's guide, I guess the tl;dr would be to read it and use it as reference, since it is a very curated and well-respected source of information.

thread_title: Need help with arc 1 final boss

content:
are you talking about 9-12? it has been a while since I last played it, but iirc the boss messes with your hand quite a bit, so having Castor as your only source of defense seems a bit iffy. Also, if Arachne is your dps, it also means that you'll run out of steam if you don't have any healing sources. 

I'm sure somebody else with a fresher memory can give more nuanced advice, but I'd recommend bringing some hp healing and weakness source like Doll or Celeste. Faint should also go hard you support her with aliemus. Just keep in mind that it is a really long fight (around +20 turns). You can also check the record list to see clears of other users (you can filter so that only clears with your awakeners show up).
thread_title: Need help with arc 1 final boss

content: Also, for phase 1, you are running Arachne dps and the bosses' hp is pretty small at the beginning. You could set up her exalt and stacks during the mob fights, then annihilate or ultra round once you enter the boss and nuke it through pursuits. It doesn't gain inmunity either, just temporary fortress of which Arachne does not care about.
thread_title: Need help with arc 1 final boss

content:
Thanks for reply
That's the thing I setup Arachne before boss and can oneshot boss on turn 1 (damage-wise), but in phase 1 he has a mechanic that, when his takes "fatal dmg",  dispells all debuffs and heals him to full hp. 
So I was wondering on how to get to phase 2 and if it's possible to cheese him with Arachne (since I'm kinda tired of killing him throughout the chapter at this point).

I will look at previous records for sure tho (completely forgot about this feature).
thread_title: Need help with arc 1 final boss

content: I've watched a record to refresh my memory, and the game itself tells you at the start of the battle that you need to stall him and break hp bars to change his attack pattern. At turn 7 it will ready a big attack and transition to phase 2

content: Level all the cards to 3, then only level further the cards and exalts that scale well and is relevant to the character's gameplan (if leveling rouse only gives aliemus on use, you can ignore it for the time being)

thread_title: Need help for end Chpt 8 Story. Do I need to swap or reinvest in new members?

content:
No it's not a skill issue on your part for not having enough heal.  If you are set on using the team you posted above, then you need to put a wheel which increases Caucus counter+heal . Rouse caucus first to ensure you get heal everytime to play his cards and stack counters. 

In my humble opinion, your skill cards are a bit under levelled. Arachne needs her 1-cost cards played . You might wanna upgrade them to generate enough alimus . Use KEYFLARE regen and damage amp. wods and cov on gmurphy.  You want gmurphy to exalt and also want red stance tents . This in turn will eat through your tents. So you need tent generation. 

Another tip.  Use your LEX. Orisons on proper cards . The enemies will stack insane shields and will keep mutate your card's costs . Choose your relics based on your play style.

I will post my team comp and wheels and covs I used .

thread_title: Need help for end Chpt 8 Story. Do I need to swap or reinvest in new members?

content: R wheel are really strong in arc 1, I'm just saying to not dismiss SR wheels out the gate. Also, I would avoid running an R wheel on your main dps. You have a +12 titan, whether it is Caecus or GMurphy, if you have an appropiate SSR wheel to pair it with you should see better output.  Also, besides gluttony, you should really run Frenzy (the eye R wheel). Most cursed relics are busted.
